name: manage_issue
description:
Creates, updates, closes, or triages issues in a GitHub repository. Always returns a preview first — call with confirmed=false (default) to review, then confirmed=true to execute. Requires GitHub connector token with 'repo' scope.
Use when: user wants to create a bug report, update an issue title/body, add labels, assign someone, or close an issue on GitHub.
Scope: remote GitHub API — write operation, requires token.
Not for: creating pull requests (use create_pr), reading issue lists (use get_repo_info), or local git operations.

action.description: Action to perform. 'create': open a new issue. 'update': edit title/body. 'close': close the issue. 'label': add/replace labels and assignees.

issue_number.description: Issue number to act on. Required for update, close, and label actions.

title.description: Issue title. Required for 'create'. Optional for 'update'.

body.description: Issue body (markdown). Optional for create and update.

labels.description: Labels to apply. Example: ['bug', 'high-priority']. For 'label' action, replaces existing labels.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign. Example: ['alice', 'bob']. For 'label' action, replaces existing assignees.

confirmed.description: Must be true to execute the write. When false (default), returns a preview without making any changes. Always review the preview before setting confirmed=true.
